name: Easy Shakshuka
description: This easy shakshuka recipe features poached eggs in a flavorful tomato sauce. It comes together quickly and can be served any time of the day!

author.name: Brittany Mullins
author.jobTitle: Food Blogger, Founder, Chef, Recipe Developer
author.description: Hey there, I’m Brittany, the creator of Eating Bird Food, cookbook author, health coach and mama of two littles. Here you'll find quick and easy recipes that make healthy fun and enjoyable for you and your family!

recipeInstructions.name: Saute
recipeInstructions.text: Heat oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Add onion, bell pepper and garlic to the skillet and cook until onions are soft and fragrant — about 5 to 10 minutes.

recipeInstructions.name: Cook sauce
recipeInstructions.text: Add cumin, paprika, cayenne, curry, turmeric, salt and pepper. Give the mixture a stir and cook for about 1 minute more. Add diced tomatoes to the skillet and bring sauce to a boil. Reduce heat to a simmer and cook until the sauce thickens up a bit, about 10 minutes. Add feta cheese to the tomato mixture and stir.

recipeInstructions.name: Cook eggs
recipeInstructions.text: Crack eggs into tomato sauce. You should be able to fit 4-5 eggs in a large skillet. Cover and let the eggs cook for about 5 minutes, or until the egg whites are completely cooked through. Remove skillet from heat, uncover and let sit for a 1-2 minutes before serving.

recipeInstructions.name: Serve
recipeInstructions.text: Spoon 1-2 eggs along with a big serving of tomato sauce on to each plate. Garnish with extra feta cheese and fresh cilantro and parsley. Serve with toast, veggies or over a whole grain like quinoa or brown rice for a complete meal.

video.name: Curried Shakshuka
video.description: This curried shakshuka, a healthy one-pan dish with poached eggs in a flavorful tomato sauce, comes together quickly and can be served any time of the day!
